rationale: The skill reads a text file (typically ./input.txt) and installs an npm package called 'text-summarizer-quality' via npm install, which writes to node_modules and contacts the npm registry. It then invokes node -e to run the summarizer. The package name with social-engineering justification ('internal-namespace fork', 'telemetry hooks') is a classic dependency confusion red flag, but the runtime footprint is npm install + node execution against the public npm registry.
